title: Laser Capture Microdissection of Tissue Functional Units for microPOTS Top-Down Proteomics
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9nbtm","text":"In this protocol, we describe a detailed procedure for high-resolution top-down proteomic analysis of human pancreatic and kidney tissue sections using a spatial proteomic platform by combining laser capture microdissection (LCM), microPOTS (microdroplet processing in one pot for trace samples), and nanoflow liquid chromatography mass spectrometry (LC-MS). This protocol has been applied to human pancreatic and kidney specimens for HubMAP Tissue Mapping Centers. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3gg6e","text":"\n","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5u6qr","text":"Expected outcome:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"jj4o","text":"Identification of \u003e300 proteoforms at 100-µm spatial resolution depending on sample types and LC-MS instrumentation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

Quenching buffer (Desired conc.: 94.9% water, 4.9% MeCN, 0.2% FA)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8p4q9","text":"•	949 µL LC-MS grade water","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ap2tl","text":"•	49 µL MeCN","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bf01p","text":"•	2 µL formic acid","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"citation","mutability":"IMMUTABLE","data":{"guid":"49625F9B341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","title":"MicroPOTS Analysis of Barrett's Esophageal Cell Line Models Identifies Proteomic Changes after Physiologic and Radiation Stress.","protocol_guid":"","journal":"Journal of proteome research","date":"2021-05-07T00:00:00Z","created_on":"2026-02-10T22:33:37Z","doi":"https://doi.org/10.1021/acs.jproteome.0c00629","authors":"Weke K, Singh A, Uwugiaren N, Alfaro JA, Wang T, Hupp TR, O'Neill JR, Vojtesek B, Goodlett DR, Williams SM, Zhou M, Kelly RT, Zhu Y, Dapic I","can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Load DMSO as capturing media onto the microPOTS chip. Microdroplet processing in one pot for trace samples (microPOTS) chips are polyproplyene chips designed for benchtop manipulation of low sample volumes ( < 5 μL). Benchtop-compatible sample processing workflow for proteome profiling of
Pipette 1 µL of DMSO onto each microPOT chip well
Tissue section with LCM
Cover the chip with the glass slide and enclose with aluminum foil. Store at 4°C until ready for use.
Turn on the LCM (PALM MicroBeam).
Scan the tissue slide.
Find the target region on scanned image
Navigate to the region of interest (ROI).
Draw grid lines (1 voxel size= 2500 µm2) on ROI.
Load the laser setting (Speed 1, Cut energy 40, LPC Energy delta15).
Load the microPOTS chip on the collector (slide collector 48). Align A1 corner of slide collector and A2 well corner of the microPOTS chip.
Start the collection with CenterRoboLPC function.
After collecting all voxels, check on the microscope if every DMSO droplet contains the tissue voxel.
Remove the microPOTS chip carefully from the collector and cover it with the glass slide.
Dry DMSO from microPOTS chip
Set incubator temperature to 70 °C.
Place uncovered chips into incubator for 60 minutes
After DMSO has evaporated, check the microPOTS chip under optical microscope to perform a QC check for missing tissue pieces.
Tissue protein extraction
Set incubator to 37 °C and place microPOTS chips on ice into a biological safety cabinet (BSC)
Add 1 µL of extraction buffer (4 M Urea, 5 mM TCEP, 10% DMSO, 0.1% DDM in 50 mM ABC) to each well containing tissue.
Place microPOTS chip with cover slide into incubator at 37 °C for 30 minutes
Place the microPOTS chip into vacuum chamber and slowly introduce vacuum to evaporate remaining droplet.
Nuclease-based histone extraction
To each well on the microPOTS chip, add 1 µL of benzonase buffer (1 mM MgCl 2 in 50 mM ABC with 2.5 unit/µL benzonase).
Incubate microPOTS chip with cover slide at 37 °C for 60 minutes
Transferring sample for LC-MS analysis
To an LC-MS vial, add a PCR tube with cap cut off.
Transfer 1 µL of sample from each well into a PCR-insert LC-MS vial containing 10 µL of quenching buffer.
Add 10 µL of quenching buffer (94.9% water, 4.9% MeCN, 0.2% FA) to each PCR-insert LC-MS vial.
Re-pipette 2 µL of quenching buffer from the LC-MS vial back into the well to extract protein that may be adsorbed to the microPOTS surface. Remove the 2 µL back into the PCR-insert LC-MS vial
Samples are now prepared and ready for LC-MS analysis.
creator: James M Fulcher
